name: antlr
title: antlr
summary: language tool for constructing recognizers, compilers etc
description:
language tool for constructing recognizers, compilers etc
ANTLR, ANother Tool for Language Recognition, (formerly PCCTS) is
a language tool that provides a framework for constructing recognizers,
compilers, and translators from grammatical descriptions containing C++
or Java actions [You can use PCCTS 1.xx to generate C-based parsers].
.
Computer language translation has become a common task. While
compilers and tools for traditional computer languages (such as C
or Java) are still being built, their number is dwarfed by the thousands
of mini-languages for which recognizers and translators are being
developed. Programmers construct translators for database formats,
graphical data files (e.g., PostScript, AutoCAD), text processing
files (e.g., HTML, SGML).  ANTLR is designed to handle all of your
translation tasks.

arguments.description: launch the ParseView debugger upon parser invocation.
arguments.raw: -debug  launch the ParseView debugger upon parser invocation.

arguments.description: generate an html file from your grammar (minus actions).
arguments.raw: -html  generate an html file from your grammar (minus actions).

arguments.description: have tree parser rules call traceIn/traceOut.
arguments.raw: -traceTreeParser  have tree parser rules call traceIn/traceOut.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use runantlr because language tool for constructing recognizers, compilers etc.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use runantlr as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `java antlr.Tool or runantlr ["args"] file.g` as documented upstream for runantlr.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair runantlr with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `runantlr --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
